data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated against the AS-SHIPPED code. VERDICT: clean pairing — NO G4 cross-child finding.

This audit supersedes the prior G4 comments on this ticket (the recurring "labels_applied list-vs-int representation conflict" finding). That finding is WITHDRAWN as no longer load-bearing — it was grounded in pre-collapse code state that 33d0 has since deleted. Repo re-verification below.

REPO RE-VERIFICATION (current HEAD, post-33d0-collapse, post-2359-implementation):
- 33d0 shipped: `_apply_outbound_update` (apply_outbound.py:125) is now a thin delegator to `_mutation_to_batch_dict` + `update_one`; the old ~240-line typed-leaf body was deleted. A repo search for `labels_applied` in apply_outbound.py returns NO matches — the list-valued `labels_applied: list[str]` payload key the prior G4 audits cited as the conflict source NO LONGER EXISTS.
- 2359 shipped: the per-sub-op counts are minted FRESH in the surviving batch path as INTEGERS. applier.py:721 declares `_subop: dict[str, int] = {}` and passes it as the `subop_applied` out-param to `update_one` (applier.py:723-728). applier.py:803-805 surfaces `outcome["labels_applied"]/["comments_applied"]/["links_applied"] = _subop.get(..., 0)` — all three uniformly integer-valued (default 0).
- The canary predicate (applier.py:814-819) is `_subop.get(f"{kind}_computed",0) > 0 and _subop.get(f"{kind}_applied",0) == 0` over kinds ("labels","comments","links") — WELL-TYPED over all three because every key is an int. There is no list anywhere in the predicate's operands. The `[] != 0` / truthiness hazard the prior finding posited is structurally impossible in the shipped design.
- The dedup-skip false-positive concern (raised under G6) is resolved: `computed` is counted POST-DEDUP, asserted by test_silent_noop_canary.py::test_dedup_does_not_trip_canary (links already present → set_relationship not called → links_applied==0 AND silent_noop absent).

7-MODE WALK (this pairing):
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 2359 and 33d0 co-edit batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py, but 2359 declares `depends_on 33d0` (deps confirm link to 33d0; description "Sequencing: depends on story D … so the counts come from one path"). 33d0 (closed) collapsed to ONE production path first; 2359 then instrumented the single survivor. Both are now closed and the surviving code is internally consistent (the integer `_subop` accumulator threads from update_one into the single outcome). No unguarded shared-state hazard.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. The previously-flagged labels representation conflict is gone (see re-verification): 2359's count-based canary assumes integer counts, and the surviving applier produces integer counts for ALL THREE kinds. 2359's cited "parity with inbound links_applied" (an int, apply_inbound.py) is now an accurate model for every sub-op kind, not just links/comments. No contradiction on the shared outbound-payload contract.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 2359's only input dependency (the single consolidated applier, story D/33d0) is correctly declared via depends_on 33d0. It needs no schema/fixture input from 8937/fe57 (it instruments an existing dispatch loop), so the absence of those edges is appropriate.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 2359 (computed>0 && applied==0 canary), 33d0 (combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect), and 822a (bidirectional round-trip) are distinct intents over the same production path. 2359 declares applier consolidation (→33d0) and probe wiring (→822a) OUT consistently; 822a round-trips comments/links/parent (NOT labels) and asserts inbound↔outbound outcomes, not the `*_applied` telemetry payload, so it imposes no expectation on 2359's counters.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The 33d0→2359 ordering is captured by depends_on 33d0; collapse-then-instrument is the correct sequence and both are closed in that order.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The added outcome keys + RECON-line sub-op fields are purely additive (non-update actions carry no sub-ops, default 0). 2359 AC5 asserts the count invariant + outcome flags, not specific log strings (test_silent_noop_canary.py checks outcome["silent_noop"]/["links_applied"]/["error"], with one structural caplog WARNING check required by the warn-first AC3). No brittle downstream expectation imposed; 822a's probe audit keys on NOT-SYNCED guards, not on the batch_outcome field set.
- Residual references — BENIGN. 2359's counters/canary/warn-first flag are additive plumbing on the surviving batch path; they reference no part of the deleted typed leaf. 33d0 deleted the typed-leaf body AND its now-misleading tests, stranding nothing 2359 depends on (2359's cited "parity" is with apply_inbound's links_applied, which is intact).

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage matter (owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 2359): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. The single recurring finding from prior G4 audits (labels list-vs-int) is withdrawn as resolved by the as-shipped 33d0 collapse + 2359 integer-count design.
